label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's check each option. Option (A) says "Computers can solve all the problems people can't do." The passage mentions that computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines. It also says that scientists use large computers to solve many problems at once. However, the passage doesn't say computers can solve all the problems people can't do. It just highlights some capabilities, not all. So A might not be correct. Option (B) states "Large computers can only solve one problem at a time." The passage says the opposite: "Scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time." So B is definitely incorrect. Option (C) claims "When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything." The passage describes the process where a blind person has to open the book, put it on the scanner, and turn the page when done. So the user does need to perform actions like opening the book and turning pages. Therefore, C is incorrect. Option (D) says "The Kurzweil machine can help blind people and short-sighted children."
